summary:
Only include content strictly related to the clinical point being evaluated; do NOT add any extra information beyond what is necessary for answering the correct choice based solely upon medical facts provided by Agent1.

During laparoscopic inguinal hernia repair, inadvertent placement of a tack below and lateral to the ilio-pubis tract can injure the genital branch of the genitofemoral nerve, which innervates the anterolateral thigh, leading to postoperative pain in that area. This explains why the patient presents with thigh discomfort after surgery at such an anatomical site.  

Final Answer: C

During laparoscopic inguinal hernia repair, misplaced tacking below and laterally to the ilio-pubic tract may compress or traumatize the genital branch of the genitofemoral nerve, responsible for sensation in the antero-lateral thigh. This accounts for the reported post-operative pain in the thigh.
